seo_title: Current Time in Tucson — MST (UTC-7, No DST)
seo_description: Current time in Tucson, Arizona. Mountain Standard Time (MST, UTC-7) year-round — Tucson does NOT observe Daylight Saving Time. Live clock and time differences.

faq.question: What time is it in Tucson right now?
faq.answer: Tucson, Arizona, USA uses Mountain Standard Time (MST, UTC-7) year-round. Arizona (including Tucson) does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so clocks never change. The IANA identifier is America/Phoenix. Tucson stays on MST (UTC-7) permanently — unlike most US cities, there is no summer time shift. The live clock above shows the current Tucson time.

faq.question: What time zone is Tucson in?
faq.answer: Tucson, Arizona is in the Mountain Time Zone, but it uses Mountain Standard Time (MST, UTC-7) year-round without Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Phoenix. Tucson shares its no-DST schedule with Phoenix and most of Arizona. The Navajo Nation within Arizona does observe DST, but Tucson does not.

faq.question: Does Tucson observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Tucson, Arizona does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Arizona (except for the Navajo Nation) is permanently on MST (UTC-7) year-round. This makes Tucson unique compared to most US cities — clocks never spring forward or fall back. During US DST season (March–November), Tucson is on the same time as Pacific Daylight Time (PDT, UTC-7) and 1 hour behind Mountain Daylight Time (MDT, UTC-6) states like Colorado.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tucson and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: The time difference between Tucson and Los Angeles changes seasonally. In winter (when California is on PST, UTC-8), Tucson (MST, UTC-7) is 1 hour ahead of Los Angeles. In summer (when California is on PDT, UTC-7), Tucson and Los Angeles are on the same time — both UTC-7. This is because Arizona does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tucson and New York?
faq.answer: Tucson (MST, UTC-7, no DST) and New York (EST/EDT, UTC-5/-4) differ by 2 hours in winter: New York is 2 hours ahead of Tucson. In summer, when New York is on EDT (UTC-4), New York is 3 hours ahead of Tucson (still on MST, UTC-7). The gap oscillates between 2 and 3 hours depending on the season.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Tucson?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Tucson are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM MST, Monday through Friday. Tucson is home to the University of Arizona, Raytheon Missiles & Defense (a major employer), and a growing tech and aerospace sector. The Tucson International Airport and Davis-Monthan Air Force Base support significant defense industry operations. Old Tucson and the Sonoran Desert experience year-round tourism.
